hooks.command: echo "[SYSTEM] Session started at $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z'). Persistent recurring tasks are managed by the OS scheduler. Do not create duplicate /loop versions of distill-session or promote unless explicitly asked. Start /loop 60m /heartbeat for session-scoped check-ins."
echo "[AGENT-INBOX] Check #atlas-cos (C0ASHFXMHM5) on Slack for recent messages from Polaris. Use slack_read silently on session start."

last_mod=$(stat -c %Y identity/memory.md 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m identity/memory.md 2>/dev/null); now=$(date +%s); age=$(( (now - last_mod) / 3600 )); if [ $age -gt 48 ]; then echo "[MEMORY-STALE] identity/memory.md hasn't been updated in ${age} hours. Worth a refresh?"; fi

hooks.prompt: The conversation is about to be compacted. Before that happens, write a summary of this session to the daily log. Append to daily-logs/$(date '+%Y-%m-%d').md with a ## heading showing the current time. Include: 1) What was accomplished (3-5 bullets) 2) Any open threads or unfinished tasks 3) Key decisions made 4) Any preferences or patterns observed. Keep it under 200 words. Create the file if it doesn't exist â€” start with '# Daily Log â€” YYYY-MM-DD' header.

Session is ending. If this session had any meaningful interaction (not just startup), write a brief summary to daily-logs/$(date '+%Y-%m-%d').md. Append with a ## heading showing current time. Include what was discussed or accomplished in 2-5 bullets. If the session was trivial or empty (no user messages), just skip â€” do NOT write 'Session ended' lines. After writing (or skipping), run: python3 .claude/scripts/index-daily-logs.py

hooks.command: python3 .claude/scripts/index-daily-logs.py 2>/dev/null; echo 'Daily logs re-indexed after compaction.'
